title: paspalum sanguinale var. pabulare
intro: Paspalum sanguinale var. pabulare, also known as watered paspalum or purpletop, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as wounds, infections, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Paspalum sanguinale var. pabulare include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herb in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of Paspalum sanguinale var. pabulare can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
